compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: HOLD (coordinator directive 2026-07-09): a standing deliberate decision in docs/scale-envelope.md L40-43 ('intentionally no batched-commit fast path — it would weaken the per-write durability guarantee') directly contests this epic's premise. Pausing before landing pending user decision (override with import-only carve-out, or veto/park). B1 change #459 is already pushed to Gerrit and must be HELD (not submitted). No tickets closed. Epic decomposition + local B1 impl retained.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: B2 (6386) and B4 (66ff) plan-review now PASS. B2 mechanism finalized as a contextvar sink at the single _seam.append_event boundary (NOT kwarg-threading through 8 layers) — default None commits per-event unchanged (guardrail 1 structurally guaranteed: only the importer sets the sink); Pass 2b (links) stays per-event due to add_dependency read-after-write (reciprocal relates_to + _is_active_link TOCTOU), Pass 2e stays per-event (stateful). B1 #459 has Verified +1; LLM-Review blocked by repo-wide review-bot outage (infra veto, not code). Watcher launched for auto-retry+submit on bot recovery. docs/scale-envelope.md rewritten to the import-only carve-out.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: B1 MERGED (#459). B2 implemented (contextvar sink at _seam.append_event; importer passes 1/2a/2c/2d batched, 2b/2e per-event) + tests; pushed Gerrit #479. B4 implemented (commit-count benchmark N=3000 -> 2*ceil(3000/256)=24 commits, malformed-chunk rollback, crash-resume K=2/source_id) + tests; pushed Gerrit #480 (stacked on #479). Local: make lint + typecheck GREEN, full default suite GREEN (unit+interfaces+import 57 tests), integration-tier B4 tests GREEN. Auto-submit watcher running for the stack (submit in order, close each child after Verified +1).

compiled_state.description:
## Context
rebar's LOCAL ticket-store write path commits one git object graph per event (~3.3
commits/event on the `tickets` branch). This is the git-issue-style high-bloat pattern;
the durable way to bound growth is to create fewer objects at the source — the git-bug
`operationPack` pattern (multiple ops serialized -> one commit). Supersedes orphan e3d0
(sleek-bur-train) and folds duplicate 39c5 (grungy-grain-drawl). Discovered from the
closed bounded-git-growth epic 97e7, which addressed *reclaim* (stock gc) but left the
*growth rate* at write time out of scope.

**SCOPE (corrected — the original text was stale).** This epic is about the LOCAL ticket
store write path ONLY (`src/rebar/_store`). The Jira **reconciler**
(`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`) is OUT OF SCOPE — the original "WS-B3: reconciler
batch-apply" line was a mis-scoped artifact and is DROPPED. The reconciler's inbound path
is a REFERENCE only (proof the batch pattern is safe in this codebase — same store, same
lock, same canonical bytes): `inbound_translate._write_event_file` writes an event file
under the write lock WITHOUT committing per-event, and the run commits the accumulated
files in a batch. We do NOT modify the reconciler; the new primitive lives in `_store` and
is index-based (atomic-rename + `git add` + one `git commit`), mirroring the existing
single-event `stage_and_commit`. (We deliberately keep the porcelain add+commit-against-
the-shared-index mechanism rather than a detached-GIT_INDEX_FILE plumbing variant, so the
batch path reuses `stage_and_commit`'s exact lock/rebase-guard/unmerged-recovery code and
stays byte-identical; no second write mechanism to maintain.)

**Why batching is invariant-safe (verified).** Replay, dedup, union-merge convergence, and
SNAPSHOT compaction all key off each event's per-event UUID, NOT commit boundaries
(`reducer/_replay.py`, `_cache.py`, `_commands/compact.py`). So collapsing N commits into 1
is invisible to readers as long as each event stays its own uuid-named file (invariant I2)
and the single locked writer (I5) is preserved.

**Already shipped (do NOT rebuild).** The importer (`src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py`)
already has idempotency-by-`source_id` (`_scan_existing_source_ids`) and deferred-push (it
sets `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` and does one final push). What remains is that it still commits
one-per-event: its passes call `rebar.create_ticket`/`edit_ticket`/`link`/`comment`, each
of which flows `composer.create_core`/`_commands/_seam.py::append_event` ->
`event_append.write_and_push` (one lock + one commit per call). Those two seam functions
are the real per-event-commit bottleneck, NOT `import_ndjson.py` alone.

## Success Criteria
- A batch-commit write primitive exists in `_store` and is adopted on the importer's
  bulk path, reducing commit count on a synthetic many-event import to `ceil(events /
  chunk_size)` commits (a >=10x reduction at the default chunk of 256), with no regression
  to replay, dedup, union-merge convergence, or SNAPSHOT compaction.

## Workstreams
- **B1** — `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(items)` where `items` is a sequence of
  `(ticket_id, event_dict)`. Acquire the write lock ONCE (it is NOT re-entrant, so B1 must
  NOT loop `stage_and_commit`); validate + canonical-stage every event BEFORE the lock;
  then holding the lock, atomic-rename each temp into its I2 path, one `git add`, ONE
  `git commit`. All-or-nothing: any failure (validation, rename, add, non-recoverable
  commit) unstages AND unlinks ALL already-placed paths (not just the failing one) — the
  single-path `_unstage`/`_silent_unlink` helpers are looped over every path. The
  `_recover_from_unmerged` helper is generalized to re-add the FULL list of batch paths on
  retry (re-adding one would drop the other N-1). Add a batched `write_and_push` variant
  (one push after). Single-event path unchanged (the reversibility guarantee: the importer
  can fall back to per-event writes by not calling B1).
- **B2** — adopt B1 on the importer's INDEPENDENT passes (Pass 1 CREATE, Passes 2a–2d
  parents/links/file-impact/comments). Bridge the interface gap explicitly: add a
  compose-ONLY seam (a variant of `create_core`/`append_event` that RETURNS the composed
  `(ticket_id, event_dict)` without committing), buffer the composed events per pass, and
  flush each buffer through `batch_stage_and_commit` in chunks. Chunk size default = 256,
  hard-coded (NO new config key — single consumer, no second call-site). Pass 2e
  (statuses) is STATEFUL (children-before-parents, `cascade=False`) → left per-event.
- **B4** — benchmark + crash-resume. A synthetic N-event import asserts commit count drops
  from ~N to `ceil(N/256)`. A malformed event mid-chunk must trigger whole-chunk rollback
  (no partial commit). A simulated crash mid-batch (kill before commit) must leave either
  the whole batch's commit or none; the next run's `source_id` re-scan skips already-
  committed events and re-emits the rest — proving partial-batch recovery. Named regression
  suites that must stay green: `tests/unit/test_store_batch_write.py`, the replay/dedup/
  union-merge tests under `tests/unit` + `tests/interfaces/store`, and the SNAPSHOT
  compaction tests.
- **(optional stretch)** — cascade transition/claim batching (parent-first cascade emits
  one commit per ancestor; stateful, medium risk). Note only; build only if B1/B2 land
  easily.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(items)`: one lock acquire, N uuid-named event files (I2), one commit; single-event write path unchanged
- [ ] All-or-nothing rollback: on any failure ALL already-renamed paths are unstaged from the index AND unlinked from the worktree; `_recover_from_unmerged` re-adds the full batch path list on unmerged-recovery retry
- [ ] Batched `write_and_push` variant (one push after the batch commit)
- [ ] Importer adopts B1 via a compose-only seam: independent passes (1, 2a–2d) buffer composed events and flush through B1 in 256-event chunks; Pass 2e (statuses) stays per-event
- [ ] Commit-count reduced to `ceil(events / 256)` on a synthetic many-event import (before/after benchmark asserts a >=10x reduction), exercised via a real import run producing a real tickets branch
- [ ] No regression: `tests/unit/test_store_batch_write.py`, replay/dedup/union-merge convergence, and SNAPSHOT compaction tests green
- [ ] Crash-resume test: partial-batch recovery leaves whole-commit-or-none and the source_id re-scan resumes cleanly; a malformed-event-mid-chunk test proves whole-chunk rollback
- [ ] Docs updated: the `docs/scale-envelope.md` "no batched-commit fast path" assertion is corrected, and an ADR captures the batch-commit decision, the I2/I5 invariants it preserves, and the deliberate reconciler-path exclusion
compiled_state.title: Write-time op batching — a batched-commit primitive for the LOCAL ticket store + bulk-path adoption
